goal: Build Atlas into a layered Termux command center that can diagnose, repair, automate, build websites, build Android app projects, manage GitHub Actions APK builds, use RAG/scope/journal for memory, and operate safely one verified step at a time.
state: Atlas is being upgraded into an automation factory for Termux repair, project indexing, file organization, Android/GitHub Actions builds, website workflows, RAG memory, journaled fixes, and safe AI-agent style task queues.
decisions: Use layered workflow for all complex tasks: READONLY scan first, PLAN second, PATCH with backup third, EXECUTE only when safe, VERIFY with command output, DEPLOY only after explicit approval.

File cleanup rule: only exact same-hash obvious duplicates from Download-style clutter may be quarantined automatically. Active project folders, protected files, media, legal/family docs, renamed APKs, source code, configs, and non-Download paths require manual review. Never delete before quarantine verification and explicit approval.

Atlas automation architecture: use deterministic scripts for scans/builds/cleanup, RAG for searchable memory, scope for project decisions, journal for completed work, GitHub Actions for Android builds when local SDK is missing, and explicit approval for deploy/delete/destructive operations.
todos: Create stable Atlas app-factory workflow commands for: project scan, focused file extraction, AI task intake, GitHub Actions build trigger, artifact download, APK install/test, website scaffold/deploy, and repair logging.
Build Atlas automation commands in this order: atlas-doctor, atlas-project-scan, atlas-rag-sync, atlas-build-commander, atlas-task-queue, atlas-swarm-runner.
